ko.original:
Seven masters—before they were geniuses,
they were wanderers searching for their own language.

Van Gogh left 900 works in 10 years,
and his stars still swirl today.

Klimt hid love and death beneath gold,
Munch screamed his loss throughout his life.

Matisse kept the joy of color even through war,
Chagall flew through the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a broken body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic panel.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds—now it's your turn to respond.
ko.translated:
7인의 거장, 그들은 천재이기 전에
자기만의 언어를 찾아 헤매던 사람들이었습니다.

고흐는 10년간 900점을 남기고 떠났고,
그의 별은 지금도 소용돌이칩니다.

클림트는 황금 아래 사랑과 죽음을 숨겼고,
뭉크는 상실의 비명을 평생 토해냈습니다.

마티스는 전쟁 속에서도 색채의 기쁨을 지켰고,
샤갈은 사랑하는 이와 함께 하늘을 날았습니다.

프리다는 부서진 몸으로 자신을 마주했고,
리히텐슈타인은 만화 한 컷으로 세상에 질문했습니다.

그들은 묻고, 부수고, 다시 세웠습니다.
일곱 개의 세계, 이제 당신이 응답합니다.
ko.back:
Before they were geniuses, these seven masters were
they were searching for a language of their own.

Van Gogh left 900 paintings in 10 years,
and his star still swirls.

Klimt hid love and death under gold,
and Munch let out a lifelong scream of loss.

Matisse kept the joy of color through war,
Chagall flew in the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a shattered body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world in a single cartoon.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds, now you respond.

ko.original:
Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotion directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, layering thick paint, with brushstrokes swirling intensely.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his canvases.
Every brushstroke carries his passion and solitude.
ko.translated:
반 고흐는 감정을 색채로 직접 드러낸 화가입니다.
물감을 두껍게 쌓아 올리는 임파스토 기법을 사용했고, 붓터치는 소용돌이치듯 격렬합니다.

노랑과 파랑의 강렬한 보색 대비가 화면을 지배합니다.
모든 붓자국에 그의 격정과 고독이 고스란히 담겨 있습니다.
ko.back:
Van Gogh was a painter who expressed his emotions directly in color.
He used the impasto technique of layering paint thickly, and his brushstrokes are swirling and violent.

Intense complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate the canvas.
Every brushstroke conveys his intensity and solitude.

ko.original:
Klimt was an artist who painted the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles, and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Beneath that gold, sensuality and divinity intertwine as one.
ko.translated:
클림트는 세기말 빈의 황금빛 꿈을 그린 화가입니다.
실제 금박을 캔버스에 입혔고, 비잔틴 모자이크에서 영감받은 기하학적 패턴이 반복됩니다.

나선형 곡선과 원형, 삼각형이 화면 전체에 장식적 리듬을 만들어냅니다.
그 황금빛 아래 관능과 신성이 하나로 얽혀 있습니다.
ko.back:
Klimt painted The Golden Dream of Vienna at the turn of the century.
Real gold leaf is applied to the canvas, and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics are repeated.

Spiraling curves, circles, and triangles create a decorative rhythm across the canvas.
Under that golden glow, sensuality and divinity are intertwined.

ko.original:
Munch painted not what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through swirling curves and distorted forms.

Blood-red skies and sickly colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
From his brush, inner screams burst forth.
ko.translated:
뭉크는 보이는 것이 아닌, 느끼는 것을 그린 화가입니다.
소용돌이치는 곡선과 왜곡된 형태로 인간의 심리를 직접 드러냅니다.

핏빛 하늘과 병적인 색채가 실존의 불안과 공포를 시각화합니다.
그의 붓끝에서 내면의 비명이 터져 나옵니다.
ko.back:
Munch was a painter who painted what he felt, not what he saw.
His swirling curves and distorted forms speak directly to the human psyche.

Blood red skies and morbid colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
Inner screams erupt from the tip of his brush.

ko.original:
Matisse was an artist who liberated color as the language of emotion.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly placing greens, purples, and oranges on faces.

Complex forms are daringly simplified into curves and flat planes.
In his paintings, color sings on its own.
ko.translated:
마티스는 색채를 감정의 언어로 해방시킨 화가입니다.
현실의 색을 따르지 않고, 얼굴에도 녹색과 보라, 주황을 거침없이 놓았습니다.

복잡한 형태는 단순한 곡선과 평면으로 과감히 생략됩니다.
그의 화면에서 색채는 스스로 노래합니다.
ko.back:
Matisse was a painter who liberated color as a language of emotion.
He didn't conform to the colors of reality, and he let greens, purples, and oranges run wild on his faces.

Complex shapes are abandoned in favor of simple curves and planes.
On his canvases, colors sing for themselves.

ko.original:
Chagall was an artist who captured love and dreams on canvas.
He painted worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue wrap around memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.
ko.translated:
샤갈은 사랑과 꿈을 화폭에 담은 화가입니다.
몽환적인 색채와 비현실적인 구성으로 기억과 환상의 세계를 그렸습니다.

분홍과 연보라, 코발트 블루가 고향 비테프스크의 기억을 감쌉니다.
그의 화면에서 현실과 꿈은 경계 없이 어우러집니다.
ko.back:
Chagall was a painter who captured love and dreams on canvas.
With dreamy colors and surreal compositions, he painted worlds of memory and fantasy.

Pinks, mauves, and cobalt blues envelop the memories of his hometown of Vitebsk.
On his canvases, reality and dreams blend together without boundaries.

ko.original:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founder of Cubism.
He deconstructed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple viewpoints on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where front and side views exist simultaneously.
He dismantled 500 years of perspective's established order.
ko.translated:
피카소는 입체주의를 창시한 20세기 최대의 혁명가입니다.
대상을 기하학적 면으로 분해하고, 여러 시점을 하나의 화면에 재조립했습니다.

정면과 측면이 동시에 존재하는 다시점 구조가 특징입니다.
그는 500년간 이어진 원근법의 질서를 해체했습니다.
ko.back:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founding cubism.
He deconstructed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple perspectives into a single view.

It is characterized by a diptych structure, where the front and side exist simultaneously.
He dismantled the 500-year-old order of perspective.

ko.original:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who transformed pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Red, yellow, and green Mexican folk colors dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.
ko.translated:
프리다 칼로는 고통을 예술로 승화시킨 화가입니다.
정면을 응시하는 강렬한 눈빛과 상징적 요소들로 자신을 마주했습니다.

빨강과 노랑, 초록의 멕시코 민속 색채가 화면을 지배합니다.
그녀의 캔버스에서 고통과 존재는 하나가 됩니다.
ko.back:
Frida Kahlo is a painter who turned suffering into art.
She confronted herself with intense stares and symbolism.

Mexican folk colors of red, yellow, and green dominate the canvas.
On her canvases, pain and existence become one.

ko.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged Ben-Day dots from print media and fixed forms with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill his canvases.
He broke down the boundary between popular culture and fine art.
ko.translated:
리히텐슈타인은 만화를 예술로 끌어올린 팝아트의 거장입니다.
인쇄물의 벤데이 도트를 거대하게 확대하고, 굵은 검은 윤곽선으로 형태를 고정했습니다.

빨강, 파랑, 노랑의 순수한 원색만이 화면을 채웁니다.
그는 대중문화와 순수미술의 경계를 허물었습니다.
ko.back:
Lichtenstein was a master of Pop Art who elevated comics to art.
He enlarged the Benday dots in the print to enormous proportions and anchored the shapes with bold black outlines.

Only the pure primary colors of red, blue, and yellow fill the screen.
He blurred the lines between pop culture and fine art.

ja.original:
Seven masters—before they were geniuses,
they were wanderers searching for their own language.

Van Gogh left 900 works in 10 years,
and his stars still swirl today.

Klimt hid love and death beneath gold,
Munch screamed his loss throughout his life.

Matisse kept the joy of color even through war,
Chagall flew through the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a broken body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic panel.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds—now it's your turn to respond.
ja.translated:
7人の巨匠、彼らは天才である前に、
自分だけの言葉を探してさまよった人たちでした。

ゴッホは10年で900点を残して旅立ち、
彼の星は今なお渦巻いています。

クリムトは黄金の下に愛と死を隠し、
ムンクは喪失の叫びを生涯にわたり吐き出しました。

マティスは戦火の中でも色彩の喜びを守り抜き、
シャガールは愛する人と空を飛びました。

フリーダは砕けた身体で自分自身と向き合い、
リキテンスタインは漫画のひとコマで世界に問いかけました。

彼らは問い、壊し、再び築き上げました。
七つの世界、次はあなたが応えます。
ja.back:
Seven masters, before they were geniuses,
they were wanderers in search of their own unique language.

Van Gogh departed after 10 years, leaving behind 900 pieces,
His star still swirls.

Klimt hid love and death beneath gold,
Munch vented his cries of loss throughout his life.

Matisse defended the joy of color in the fires of war,
Chagall flew with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with her shattered body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic strip.

They questioned, broke down, and built up again.
Seven worlds, you will answer next.

ja.original:
Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotion directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, layering thick paint, with brushstrokes swirling intensely.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his canvases.
Every brushstroke carries his passion and solitude.
ja.translated:
ファン・ゴッホは感情を色彩で直接さらけ出した画家です。
絵の具を厚く塗り重ねるインパスト技法を用い、筆致は渦巻くように激しく躍動します。

黄色と青の強烈な補色対比が画面を支配しています。
すべての筆跡に、彼の情熱と孤独がそのまま宿っています。
ja.back:
Van Gogh is a painter who directly exposes his emotions through color.
He used the impasto technique, in which thick layers of paint are applied, and his brush strokes are swirling, intense, and dynamic.

The strong complementary color contrast of yellow and blue dominates the painting.
His passion and solitude are present in every brushstroke.

ja.original:
Klimt was an artist who painted the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles, and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Beneath that gold, sensuality and divinity intertwine as one.
ja.translated:
クリムトは世紀末ウィーンの黄金の夢を描いた画家です。
実際の金箔をカンヴァスに施し、ビザンティン・モザイクから霊感を得た幾何学的パターンが繰り返されます。

螺旋形の曲線と円形、三角形が画面全体に装飾的なリズムを生み出しています。
その黄金色の下に、官能と神聖が一つに絡み合っています。
ja.back:
Klimt was the painter of the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
Actual gold leaf is applied to the canvas and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics are repeated.

Spiraling curves, circles and triangles create a decorative rhythm throughout the painting.
Beneath the golden hues, the sensual and the sacred are intertwined into one.

ja.original:
Munch painted not what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through swirling curves and distorted forms.

Blood-red skies and sickly colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
From his brush, inner screams burst forth.
ja.translated:
ムンクは見えるものではなく、感じるものを描いた画家です。
渦巻く曲線と歪んだ形態で、人間の心理を直接さらけ出しています。

血の色の空と病んだ色彩が、実存の不安と恐怖を視覚化しています。
彼の筆先から内面の叫びが噴き出しています。
ja.back:
Munch was a painter who painted what he felt, not what he saw.
With his swirling curves and distorted forms, he directly exposes the human psyche.

Blood-colored skies and sickly colors visualize existential angst and dread.
An inner cry erupts from his brushstrokes.

ja.original:
Matisse was an artist who liberated color as the language of emotion.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly placing greens, purples, and oranges on faces.

Complex forms are daringly simplified into curves and flat planes.
In his paintings, color sings on its own.
ja.translated:
マティスは色彩を感情の言語として解放した画家です。
現実の色に従わず、顔にも緑や紫、オレンジを躊躇なく置きました。

複雑な形態は単純な曲線と平面に大胆に省略されています。
彼の画面で色彩はみずから歌い出します。
ja.back:
Matisse was a painter who liberated color as a language of emotion.
He did not follow the colors of reality and did not hesitate to place greens, purples, and oranges on faces.

Complex forms are boldly omitted in favor of simple curves and planes.
In his paintings, color sings itself out.

ja.original:
Chagall was an artist who captured love and dreams on canvas.
He painted worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue wrap around memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.
ja.translated:
シャガールは愛と夢を画布に描いた画家です。
夢幻的な色彩と非現実的な構成で、記憶と幻想の世界を描きました。

ピンクと淡い紫、コバルトブルーが故郷ヴィテブスクの記憶を包み込んでいます。
彼の画面で現実と夢は境界なく溶け合っています。
ja.back:
Chagall was a painter who painted love and dreams on canvas.
With dreamy colors and unrealistic compositions, he painted a world of memory and fantasy.

Pink, pale purple, and cobalt blue envelop the memory of his hometown of Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.

ja.original:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founder of Cubism.
He deconstructed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple viewpoints on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where front and side views exist simultaneously.
He dismantled 500 years of perspective's established order.
ja.translated:
ピカソはキュビスムを創始した20世紀最大の革命家です。
対象を幾何学的な面に分解し、複数の視点を一つの画面に再構成しました。

正面と側面が同時に存在する多視点構造が特徴です。
彼は500年続いた遠近法の秩序を解体しました。
ja.back:
Picasso is the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century who founded cubism.
He decomposed his subjects into geometric surfaces and recomposed multiple perspectives into a single picture plane.

The multi-viewpoint structure is characterized by the simultaneous presence of the front and the side.
He dismantled the order of perspective that had lasted for 500 years.

ja.original:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who transformed pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Red, yellow, and green Mexican folk colors dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.
ja.translated:
フリーダ・カーロは痛みを芸術に昇華させた画家です。
正面を見据える強烈な眼差しと象徴的な要素で自分自身と向き合いました。

赤と黄、緑のメキシコの民俗色彩が画面を支配しています。
彼女のカンヴァスで痛みと存在は一つになっています。
ja.back:
Frida Kahlo is a painter who sublimated pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze looking straight ahead and symbolic elements.

Mexican folk colors of red, yellow, and green dominate the painting.
Pain and existence are one in her canvases.

ja.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged Ben-Day dots from print media and fixed forms with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill his canvases.
He broke down the boundary between popular culture and fine art.
ja.translated:
リキテンスタインは漫画を芸術に引き上げたポップアートの巨匠です。
印刷物のベンデイ・ドットを巨大に拡大し、太い黒の輪郭線で形を固定しました。

赤、青、黄の純粋な原色だけが画面を満たしています。
彼は大衆文化と純粋芸術の境界を壊しました。
ja.back:
Lichtenstein was a master of Pop Art who elevated cartooning to an art form.
He enlarged the printed benday dots to enormous sizes and fixed the shapes with thick black outlines.

Only the pure primary colors of red, blue, and yellow fill the screen.
He broke the boundary between popular culture and pure art.

es.original:
Seven masters—before they were geniuses,
they were wanderers searching for their own language.

Van Gogh left 900 works in 10 years,
and his stars still swirl today.

Klimt hid love and death beneath gold,
Munch screamed his loss throughout his life.

Matisse kept the joy of color even through war,
Chagall flew through the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a broken body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic panel.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds—now it's your turn to respond.

Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotion directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, layering thick paint, with brushstrokes swirling intensely.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his canvases.
Every brushstroke carries his passion and solitude.
es.translated:
Van Gogh fue un pintor que reveló sus emociones directamente a través del color.
Usaba la técnica del empaste, apilando pintura gruesa, y sus pinceladas se arremolinan con violencia.

El intenso contraste complementario entre amarillo y azul domina el lienzo.
En cada trazo de pincel queda grabada, intacta, su pasión y su soledad.
es.back:
Van Gogh was a painter who revealed his emotions directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, piling up thick paint, and his brushstrokes swirl violently.

The intense complementary contrast between yellow and blue dominates the canvas.
In each brush stroke is engraved, intact, his passion and his loneliness.

es.original:
Klimt was an artist who painted the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles, and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Beneath that gold, sensuality and divinity intertwine as one.
es.translated:
Klimt fue el pintor que retrató el sueño dorado de la Viena del fin de siglo.
Aplicaba verdadero pan de oro sobre el lienzo, y los patrones geométricos inspirados en mosaicos bizantinos se repiten.

Espirales, círculos y triángulos crean un ritmo decorativo en toda la superficie.
Bajo ese brillo dorado, la sensualidad y lo sagrado se entrelazan.
es.back:
Klimt was the painter who portrayed the golden dream of turn-of-the-century Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf on canvas, and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics are repeated.

Spirals, circles and triangles create a decorative rhythm across the surface.
Beneath that golden glow, sensuality and the sacred are intertwined.

es.original:
Munch painted not what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through swirling curves and distorted forms.

Blood-red skies and sickly colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
From his brush, inner screams burst forth.
es.translated:
Munch fue un pintor que no pintó lo que se ve, sino lo que se siente.
Con curvas en espiral y formas distorsionadas, reveló la psicología humana directamente.

Cielos color sangre y colores enfermizos visualizan la angustia existencial y el terror.
De la punta de su pincel brota el grito interior.
es.back:
Munch was a painter who painted not what is seen, but what is felt.
With spiraling curves and distorted forms, he revealed human psychology directly.

Blood-colored skies and sickly colors visualize existential anguish and terror.
From the tip of his brush sprouts the inner scream.

es.original:
Matisse was an artist who liberated color as the language of emotion.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly placing greens, purples, and oranges on faces.

Complex forms are daringly simplified into curves and flat planes.
In his paintings, color sings on its own.
es.translated:
Matisse fue un pintor que liberó el color como lenguaje de las emociones.
No siguió el color real: puso verde, violeta y naranja sin vacilar incluso sobre los rostros.

Las formas complejas se simplifican con audacia en curvas y planos.
En sus lienzos, el color canta por sí mismo.
es.back:
Matisse was a painter who liberated color as a language of emotions.
He did not follow the real color: he put green, violet and orange without hesitation even on faces.

Complex forms are boldly simplified in curves and planes.
In his canvases, color sings for itself.

es.original:
Chagall was an artist who captured love and dreams on canvas.
He painted worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue wrap around memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.
es.translated:
Chagall fue un pintor que volcó amor y sueños sobre el lienzo.
Con colores de ensueño y composiciones irreales, pintó mundos de recuerdos y fantasía.

Rosas, lavandas y azul cobalto envuelven los recuerdos de su Vitebsk natal.
En sus lienzos, la realidad y el sueño se funden sin fronteras.
es.back:
Chagall was a painter who poured love and dreams on canvas.
With dreamy colors and unreal compositions, he painted worlds of memories and fantasy.

Roses, lavender and cobalt blue envelop the memories of his native Vitebsk.
In his canvases, reality and dream merge without borders.

es.original:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founder of Cubism.
He deconstructed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple viewpoints on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where front and side views exist simultaneously.
He dismantled 500 years of perspective's established order.
es.translated:
Picasso fue el mayor revolucionario del siglo XX, fundador del Cubismo.
Descompuso los objetos en planos geométricos y reensamblaba múltiples perspectivas en un solo lienzo.

La estructura de perspectiva múltiple, donde frente y perfil coexisten, es su sello.
Destruyó 500 años de orden perspectivo.
es.back:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founder of Cubism.
He decomposed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple perspectives on a single canvas.

The structure of multiple perspective, where front and profile coexist, is his hallmark.
He destroyed 500 years of perspectival order.

es.original:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who transformed pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Red, yellow, and green Mexican folk colors dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.
es.translated:
Frida Kahlo fue una artista que sublimó el dolor en arte.
Con una mirada frontal intensa y elementos simbólicos, se enfrentó a sí misma.

Los rojos, amarillos y verdes del folclore mexicano dominan el lienzo.
En su lienzo, dolor y existencia se hacen uno.
es.back:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who sublimated pain into art.
With an intense frontal gaze and symbolic elements, she confronted herself.

The reds, yellows and greens of Mexican folklore dominate the canvas.
In her canvas, pain and existence become one.

es.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged Ben-Day dots from print media and fixed forms with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill his canvases.
He broke down the boundary between popular culture and fine art.
es.translated:
Lichtenstein fue un maestro del Pop Art que elevó el cómic a la categoría de arte.
Amplió a gran escala los puntos Ben-Day de la impresión y fijó las formas con gruesos contornos negros.

Solo los primarios puros — rojo, azul y amarillo — llenan el lienzo.
Borró la frontera entre cultura popular y bellas artes.
es.back:
Lichtenstein was a master of Pop Art who elevated comics to the status of art.
He enlarged the Ben-Day dots of the print on a large scale and fixed the shapes with thick black outlines.

Only pure primaries - red, blue and yellow - fill the canvas.
He blurred the boundary between popular culture and fine art.

fr.original:
Seven masters—before they were geniuses,
they were wanderers searching for their own language.

Van Gogh left 900 works in 10 years,
and his stars still swirl today.

Klimt hid love and death beneath gold,
Munch screamed his loss throughout his life.

Matisse kept the joy of color even through war,
Chagall flew through the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a broken body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic panel.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds—now it's your turn to respond.

Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotion directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, layering thick paint, with brushstrokes swirling intensely.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his canvases.
Every brushstroke carries his passion and solitude.
fr.translated:
Van Gogh fut un peintre qui révéla ses émotions directement par la couleur.
Il utilisait la technique de l'empâtement, superposant la peinture en couches épaisses, et ses touches tourbillonnent avec violence.

L'intense contraste complémentaire entre jaune et bleu domine la toile.
Dans chaque coup de pinceau, sa passion et sa solitude sont gravées intactes.
fr.back:
Van Gogh was a painter who revealed his emotions directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, superimposing paint in thick layers, and his strokes swirl with violence.

The intense complementary contrast between yellow and blue dominates the canvas.
In every brushstroke, his passion and solitude are etched intact.

fr.original:
Klimt was an artist who painted the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles, and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Beneath that gold, sensuality and divinity intertwine as one.
fr.translated:
Klimt fut le peintre qui peignit le rêve doré de la Vienne fin de siècle.
Il appliquait de véritables feuilles d'or sur la toile, et les motifs géométriques inspirés des mosaïques byzantines se répètent.

Spirales, cercles et triangles créent un rythme décoratif sur toute la surface.
Sous cet éclat doré, sensualité et sacralité s'entrelacent.
fr.back:
Klimt was the painter who painted the golden dream of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to the canvas, and the geometric motifs inspired by Byzantine mosaics are repeated.

Spirals, circles and triangles create a decorative rhythm across the entire surface.
Under this golden glow, sensuality and sacredness intertwine.

fr.original:
Munch painted not what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through swirling curves and distorted forms.

Blood-red skies and sickly colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
From his brush, inner screams burst forth.
fr.translated:
Munch fut un peintre qui ne peignit pas ce que l'on voit, mais ce que l'on ressent.
Par des courbes en spirale et des formes distordues, il révéla la psychologie humaine directement.

Des ciels couleur de sang et des couleurs maladives visualisent l'angoisse existentielle et la terreur.
De la pointe de son pinceau jaillit le cri intérieur.
fr.back:
Munch was a painter who painted not what we see, but what we feel.
Through spiraling curves and distorted forms, he revealed human psychology directly.

Blood-colored skies and sickly colors visualize existential anguish and terror.
From the tip of his brush springs the inner cry.

fr.original:
Matisse was an artist who liberated color as the language of emotion.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly placing greens, purples, and oranges on faces.

Complex forms are daringly simplified into curves and flat planes.
In his paintings, color sings on its own.
fr.translated:
Matisse fut un peintre qui libéra la couleur comme langage des émotions.
Il ne suivit pas la couleur réelle : il posa du vert, du violet et de l'orange sans hésiter même sur les visages.

Les formes complexes sont simplifiées avec audace en courbes et en aplats.
Sur ses toiles, la couleur chante d'elle-même.
fr.back:
Matisse was a painter who liberated color as the language of emotions.
He did not follow actual color: he used green, violet and orange without hesitation, even on faces.

Complex shapes are boldly simplified in curves and flat tints.
On his canvases, color sings for itself.

fr.original:

Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue wrap around memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.

Chagall fut un peintre qui versa l'amour et les rêves sur la toile.

Roses, lavandes et bleu cobalt enveloppent les souvenirs de son Vitebsk natal.
Sur ses toiles, réalité et rêve se fondent sans frontière.
fr.back:

Roses, lavender and cobalt blue envelop memories of his native Vitebsk.
On his canvases, reality and dreams merge without borders.

Bentuk kompleks disederhanakan dengan berani menjadi kurva dan bidang datar.
Di atas kanvasnya, warna bernyanyi sendiri.
id.back:
Matisse was a painter who liberated color as a language of emotion.
He did not follow real colors: he put green, purple, and orange even on the face without hesitation.

Complex shapes are boldly simplified into curves and flat planes.
On his canvases, colors sing on their own.

id.original:
Chagall was an artist who captured love and dreams on canvas.
He painted worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue wrap around memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.
id.translated:
Chagall adalah pelukis yang menuangkan cinta dan mimpi ke atas kanvas.
Melalui warna mimpi dan komposisi tak nyata, ia melukis dunia kenangan dan fantasi.

Merah muda, lavender, dan biru kobalt menyelimuti kenangan Vitebsk kampung halamannya.
Di atas kanvasnya, kenyataan dan mimpi melebur tanpa batas.
id.back:
Chagall was a painter who poured love and dreams onto canvas.
Through dreamy colors and unreal compositions, he painted a world of memories and fantasies.

Pink, lavender and cobalt blue envelop the memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
On her canvases, reality and dreams merge seamlessly.

id.original:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founder of Cubism.
He deconstructed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple viewpoints on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where front and side views exist simultaneously.
He dismantled 500 years of perspective's established order.
id.translated:
Picasso adalah revolusioner terbesar abad ke-20, pendiri Kubisme.
Ia mengurai objek menjadi bidang geometris dan merangkai ulang beberapa perspektif di satu kanvas.

Struktur multi-perspektif, di mana depan dan samping berdampingan, adalah ciri khasnya.
Ia menghancurkan 500 tahun tatanan perspektif.
id.back:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, the founder of Cubism.
He broke down objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple perspectives on a single canvas.

The multi-perspective structure, where front and side are side by side, is his trademark.
He destroyed 500 years of perspective order.

id.original:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who transformed pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Red, yellow, and green Mexican folk colors dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.
id.translated:
Frida Kahlo adalah seniman yang menyublimkan rasa sakit menjadi seni.
Melalui tatapan frontal intens dan elemen simbolis, ia berhadapan dengan dirinya sendiri.

Merah, kuning, dan hijau folklor Meksiko mendominasi kanvas.
Di atas kanvasnya, rasa sakit dan eksistensi menyatu.
id.back:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who sublimated pain into art.
Through intense frontal gazes and symbolic elements, she confronted herself.

The red, yellow and green of Mexican folklore dominate the canvas.
On her canvases, pain and existence merge.

id.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged Ben-Day dots from print media and fixed forms with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill his canvases.
He broke down the boundary between popular culture and fine art.
id.translated:
Lichtenstein adalah maestro Pop Art yang mengangkat komik ke tingkat seni.
Ia memperbesar titik-titik Ben-Day dari cetakan ke skala besar dan mempertahankan bentuk dengan kontur hitam tebal.

Hanya warna primer murni — merah, biru, dan kuning — yang mengisi kanvas.
Ia menghapus batas antara budaya populer dan seni rupa.
id.back:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art maestro who elevated comics to the level of art.
He enlarged Ben-Day's dots from the print to a large scale and preserved the shape with thick black contours.

Only pure primary colors - red, blue, and yellow - filled the canvas.
He erased the boundaries between popular culture and fine art.

ar.original:
Seven masters—before they were geniuses,
they were wanderers searching for their own language.

Van Gogh left 900 works in 10 years,
and his stars still swirl today.

Klimt hid love and death beneath gold,
Munch screamed his loss throughout his life.

Matisse kept the joy of color even through war,
Chagall flew through the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a broken body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic panel.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds—now it's your turn to respond.
ar.translated:
سبعة أساتذة — قبل أن يصبحوا عباقرة،
كانوا تائهين يبحثون عن لغتهم الخاصة.

فان غوخ ترك 900 لوحة في 10 سنوات،ونجومه لا تزال تدور حتى اليوم.

كليمت أخفى الحب والموت تحت الذهب،
مونك صرخ فقدانه طوال حياته.

ماتيس حافظ على فرحة اللون حتى وسط الحرب،
شاغال حلّق في السماء مع حبيبته.

فريدا واجهت نفسها بجسد محطّم،
ليختنشتاين ساءل العالم بمربّع كوميكس واحد.

سألوا، حطّموا، أعادوا البناء.
سبعة عوالم — الآن جاء دورك للإجابة.
ar.back:
Seven professors - before they became geniuses.
they were wanderers looking for their own language.

Van Gogh left 900 paintings in 10 years, and his stars are still spinning today.

Klimt hid love and death under gold.
Monk screamed his loss all his life.

Matisse preserved the joy of color even in the midst of war.
Chagall flew in the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a broken body.
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comics square.

They asked, they destroyed, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds - now it's your turn to answer.

ar.original:
Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotion directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, layering thick paint, with brushstrokes swirling intensely.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his canvases.
Every brushstroke carries his passion and solitude.
ar.translated:
فان غوخ فنّان عبّر مباشرة عن المشاعر من خلال اللون.
استخدم تقنية الإمباستو، يتراكم الطلاء السميك بضربات فرشاة تتلوّى بشدّة.

تباينات مكمّلة جريئة من الأصفر والأزرق تهيمن على لوحاته.
كل ضربة فرشاة تحمل عاطفته ووحدته.
ar.back:
Van Gogh is an artist who directly expressed emotions through color.
Using the Impasto technique, thick paint builds up with brushstrokes that wriggle wildly.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his paintings.
Each brushstroke carries his emotion and loneliness.

ar.original:
Klimt was an artist who painted the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles, and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Beneath that gold, sensuality and divinity intertwine as one.
ar.translated:
كليمت فنّان رسم الأحلام الذهبية لفيينا نهاية القرن.
لصق رقائق الذهب الحقيقية على القماش، بأنماط هندسية مستلهمة من الفسيفساء البيزنطية.

المنحنيات الحلزونية والدوائر والمثلثات تخلق إيقاعات زخرفية عبر كامل السطح.
تحت ذلك الذهب، تتوحّد الشهوانية والقداسة كشيء واحد.
ar.back:
Klimt is an artist who painted the golden dreams of turn-of-the-century Vienna.
He pasted real gold foil onto canvas, in geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Beneath that gold, sensuality and holiness unite as one.

ar.original:
Munch painted not what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through swirling curves and distorted forms.

Blood-red skies and sickly colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
From his brush, inner screams burst forth.
ar.translated:
مونك لم يرسم ما رآه، بل ما شعر به.
كشف علم النفس الإنساني عبر المنحنيات المتلوّية والأشكال المشوّهة.

السماوات الدموية والألوان المريضة تُجسّد القلق الوجودي والخوف.
من فرشاته، انفجرت الصرخات الداخلية.
ar.back:
Monk did not paint what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through squiggly curves and distorted shapes.

The bloody skies and sick colors embody existential anxiety and fear.
From his brush, inner screams exploded.

ar.original:
Matisse was an artist who liberated color as the language of emotion.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly placing greens, purples, and oranges on faces.

Complex forms are daringly simplified into curves and flat planes.
In his paintings, color sings on its own.
ar.translated:
ماتيس فنّان حرّر اللون كلغة عاطفية.
رفض الألوان الواقعية، واضعاً الأخضر والبنفسجي والبرتقالي على الوجوه بجرأة.

الأشكال المعقّدة مبسّطة جرأةً في منحنيات ومسطّحات.
في لوحاته، اللون يغنّي وحده.
ar.back:
Matisse is an artist who liberated color as an emotional language.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly putting green, purple, and orange on faces.

Complex shapes are boldly simplified into curves and flats.
In his paintings, color sings alone.

ar.original:
Chagall was an artist who captured love and dreams on canvas.
He painted worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue wrap around memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.
ar.translated:
شاغال فنّان التقط الحب والأحلام على القماش.
رسم عوالم الذاكرة والخيال بألوان كالأحلام وتكوينات سريالية.

الوردي والبنفسجي الناعم والأزرق الكوبالتي تلفّ ذكريات موطنه فيتبسك.
في لوحاته، تمتزج الواقع والأحلام دون حدود.
ar.back:
Chagall is an artist who captures love and dreams on canvas.
He paints worlds of memory and imagination with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pinks, soft violets, and cobalt blues envelop the memories of his homeland.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without borders.

ar.original:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founder of Cubism.
He deconstructed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple viewpoints on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where front and side views exist simultaneously.
He dismantled 500 years of perspective's established order.
ar.translated:
بيكاسو كان أعظم ثوري في القرن العشرين، مؤسّس التكعيبية.
حلّل الأشياء إلى مسطّحات هندسية وأعاد تجميع وجهات نظر متعدّدة على قماش واحد.

بصمته هي البنية متعدّدة المنظورات حيث يتعايش الوجه والجانب في آن واحد.
حطّم النظام الراسخ للمنظور على مدى 500 عام.
ar.back:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, the founder of Cubism.
He analyzed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple perspectives on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where face and side coexist simultaneously.
He shattered the established order of perspective over 500 years.

ar.original:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who transformed pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Red, yellow, and green Mexican folk colors dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.
ar.translated:
فريدا كاهلو فنّانة حوّلت الألم إلى فن.
واجهت نفسها بنظرة مكثّفة وعناصر رمزية.

الأحمر والأصفر والأخضر من ألوان الفولكلور المكسيكي تهيمن على لوحاتها.
في لوحاتها، يصبح الألم والوجود شيئاً واحداً.
ar.back:
Frida Kahlo is an artist who turned pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

The red, yellow, and green colors of Mexican folklore dominate her paintings.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.

ar.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged Ben-Day dots from print media and fixed forms with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill his canvases.
He broke down the boundary between popular culture and fine art.
ar.translated:
ليختنشتاين كان سيد فن البوب الذي رفع الكوميكس إلى الفنون الجميلة.
كبّر نقاط بن داي من وسائل الإعلام المطبوعة وثبّت الأشكال بمحيطات سوداء جريئة.

فقط الألوان الأساسية النقية — الأحمر والأزرق والأصفر — تملأ لوحاته.
محا الحدود بين الثقافة الشعبية والفنون الجميلة.
ar.back:
Lichtenstein was a pop art master who elevated comics to fine art.
Ben Day zoomed out of print media and stabilized the figures with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors - red, blue, and yellow - filled his canvases.
He erased the boundaries between pop culture and fine art.
Vincent van Gogh (1853-1890)

Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, the founder of Cubism.
He analyzed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple perspectives on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where face and side coexist simultaneously.
He shattered the established order of perspective over 500 years.

ar.original:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who transformed pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Red, yellow, and green Mexican folk colors dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.
ar.translated:
فريدا كاهلو فنّانة حوّلت الألم إلى فن.
واجهت نفسها بنظرة مكثّفة وعناصر رمزية.

الأحمر والأصفر والأخضر من ألوان الفولكلور المكسيكي تهيمن على لوحاتها.
في لوحاتها، يصبح الألم والوجود شيئاً واحداً.
ar.back:
Frida Kahlo is an artist who turned pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

The red, yellow, and green colors of Mexican folklore dominate her paintings.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.

ar.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged Ben-Day dots from print media and fixed forms with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill his canvases.
He broke down the boundary between popular culture and fine art.
ar.translated:
ليختنشتاين كان سيد فن البوب الذي رفع الكوميكس إلى الفنون الجميلة.
كبّر نقاط بن داي من وسائل الإعلام المطبوعة وثبّت الأشكال بمحيطات سوداء جريئة.

فقط الألوان الأساسية النقية — الأحمر والأزرق والأصفر — تملأ لوحاته.
محا الحدود بين الثقافة الشعبية والفنون الجميلة.
ar.back:
Lichtenstein was a pop art master who elevated comics to fine art.
Ben Day zoomed out of print media and stabilized the figures with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors - red, blue, and yellow - filled his canvases.
He erased the boundaries between pop culture and fine art.

tr.original:
Seven masters—before they were geniuses,
they were wanderers searching for their own language.

Van Gogh left 900 works in 10 years,
and his stars still swirl today.

Klimt hid love and death beneath gold,
Munch screamed his loss throughout his life.

Matisse kept the joy of color even through war,
Chagall flew through the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a broken body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic panel.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds—now it's your turn to respond.
tr.translated:
Yedi usta — dahi olmadan önce,
kendi dillerini arayan birer serseri.

Van Gogh 10 yılda 900 eser bıraktı,
yıldızları bugün hâlâ dönüyor.

Klimt altın altında aşkı ve ölümü sakladı,
Munch kaybını ömrü boyunca çığlık attı.

Matisse savaş ortasında bile rengin sevincini korudu,
Chagall sevgilisiyle gökyüzünde uçtu.

Frida kırık bir bedenle kendisiyle yüzleşti,
Lichtenstein tek bir çizgi roman karesiyle dünyayı sorguladı.

Sordular, kırdılar, yeniden inşa ettiler.
Yedi dünya — şimdi senin cevap verme sıran.
tr.back:
Seven masters - before they became geniuses,
vagabonds in search of their own language.

Van Gogh created 900 works in 10 years,
and their stars are still spinning today.

Klimt hid love and death under gold,
Munch screamed about his loss for the rest of his life.

Matisse kept the joy of color even in the midst of war,
Chagall flew in the sky with his lover.

Frida faced herself with a broken body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic frame.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds - now it's your turn to answer.

tr.original:
Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotion directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, layering thick paint, with brushstrokes swirling intensely.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his canvases.
Every brushstroke carries his passion and solitude.
tr.translated:
Van Gogh, duyguyu doğrudan renk aracılığıyla dile getiren bir sanatçıydı.
Impasto tekniğini kullandı; kalın boya yoğun dönen fırça darbeleriyle katmanlandı.

Sarı ve mavinin cesur tamamlayıcı zıtlıkları tuvale hâkim.
Her fırça darbesi tutkusunu ve yalnızlığını taşıyor.
tr.back:
Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotion directly through color.
He used the impasto technique; thick paint layered with intense swirling brushstrokes.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate the canvas.
Each brush stroke carries her passion and loneliness.

tr.original:
Klimt was an artist who painted the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles, and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Beneath that gold, sensuality and divinity intertwine as one.
tr.translated:
Klimt, fin de siècle Viyana'sının altın düşlerini resmeden sanatçıydı.
Tuvale gerçek altın yaprak uyguladı; Bizans mozaiklerinden ilham alan geometrik desenlerle.

Spiral kıvrımlar, daireler ve üçgenler tüm yüzey boyunca dekoratif ritimler yaratıyor.
O altının altında duyusallık ve kutsallık tek bütün olarak birleşiyor.
tr.back:
Klimt was the artist who painted the golden dreams of fin de siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to the canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Under that gold, sensuality and sacredness merge into a single whole.

tr.original:
Munch painted not what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through swirling curves and distorted forms.

Blood-red skies and sickly colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
From his brush, inner screams burst forth.
tr.translated:
Munch gördüğünü değil, hissettiklerini resmetti.
Kıvrımlı eğriler ve çarpıtılmış formlar aracılığıyla insan psikolojisini ortaya koydu.

Kanlı gökyüzleri ve hastalıklı renkler varoluşsal kaygı ve korkuyu görselleştiriyor.
Fırçasından iç çığlıklar fışkırıyor.
tr.back:
Munch painted what he felt, not what he saw.
Through sinuous curves and distorted forms, he revealed human psychology.

Bloody skies and sickly colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
Inner screams emanate from her brush.

tr.original:
Matisse was an artist who liberated color as the language of emotion.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly placing greens, purples, and oranges on faces.

Complex forms are daringly simplified into curves and flat planes.
In his paintings, color sings on its own.
tr.translated:
Matisse, rengi duygunun dili olarak özgürleştiren sanatçıydı.
Gerçekçi renkleri reddederek yüzlere yeşil, mor ve turuncu cesurca yerleştirdi.

Karmaşık formlar eğriler ve düzlemler hâline cesurca basitleştiriliyor.
Tablelarında renk kendi başına şarkı söylüyor.
tr.back:
Matisse was the artist who liberated color as the language of emotion.
Rejecting realistic colors, he boldly placed green, purple and orange on faces.

Complex forms are boldly simplified into curves and planes.
In his paintings, color sings on its own.

tr.original:
Chagall was an artist who captured love and dreams on canvas.
He painted worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue wrap around memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.
tr.translated:
Chagall, aşkı ve düşleri tuvale aktaran sanatçıydı.
Hafıza ve fantezi dünyalarını düşsel renkler ve gerçeküstü kompozisyonlarla resmetti.

Pembe, yumuşak mor ve kobalt mavi anavatanı Vitebsk'in anılarını sarıyor.
Tablelarında gerçeklik ve düşler sınırsızca iç içe geçiyor.
tr.back:
Chagall was the artist who transferred love and dreams to canvas.
He painted worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamy colors and surreal compositions.

Pink, soft purple and cobalt blue surround the memories of his homeland Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams intertwine endlessly.

tr.original:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founder of Cubism.
He deconstructed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple viewpoints on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where front and side views exist simultaneously.
He dismantled 500 years of perspective's established order.
tr.translated:
Picasso, Kübizm'in kurucusu 20. yüzyılın en büyük devrimcisiydi.
Nesneleri geometrik düzlemlere ayrıştırdı ve birden fazla bakış açısını tek tuvalde yeniden derledi.

İmzası, ön ve yan görünümün aynı anda var olduğu çok perspektifli yapı.
500 yıllık perspektifin yerleşik düzenini yerle bir etti.
tr.back:
Picasso, the founder of Cubism, was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century.
He decomposed objects into geometric planes and recompiled multiple points of view on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure in which the front and side view exist simultaneously.
He overturned 500 years of perspective's established order.

tr.original:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who transformed pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Red, yellow, and green Mexican folk colors dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.
tr.translated:
Frida Kahlo, acıyı sanata dönüştüren bir sanatçıydı.
Yoğun bakışı ve sembolik öğelerle kendisiyle yüzleşti.

Kırmızı, sarı ve yeşil Meksika halk renkleri tuvale hâkim.
Tablelarında acı ve varoluş bir bütün hâline geliyor.
tr.back:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who turned pain into art.
He confronted himself with his intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Mexican folk colors of red, yellow and green dominate the canvas.
Pain and existence become one in his paintings.

tr.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged Ben-Day dots from print media and fixed forms with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill his canvases.
He broke down the boundary between popular culture and fine art.
tr.translated:
Lichtenstein, çizgi romanı güzel sanatlara yükselten Pop Art ustasıydı.
Basılı medyadan Ben-Day noktaları büyüttü ve formları kalın siyah konturlarla sabitledi.

Yalnızca saf ana renkler — kırmızı, mavi ve sarı — tuvali dolduruyor.
Popüler kültür ile güzel sanatlar arasındaki sınırı ortadan kaldırdı.
tr.back:
Lichtenstein was the Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
From the printed media, Ben-Day enlarged the dots and fixed the forms with thick black contours.

Only pure primary colors - red, blue and yellow - fill the canvas.
He broke the boundary between popular culture and fine art.

Matisse guardou a alegria das cores mesmo em tempos de guerra,
Chagall voou pelos céus com a sua amada.

Frida enfrentou a si mesma com um corpo partido,
Lichtenstein questionou o mundo com um único quadro de quadrinhos.

Perguntaram, quebraram, reconstruíram.
Sete mundos — agora é a tua vez de responder.
pt.back:
Seven masters - before they became geniuses,
were wanderers in search of their own language.

Van Gogh left 900 canvases in 10 years,
his stars are still spinning today.

Klimt hid love and death under gold,
Munch cried out his loss all his life.

Matisse kept the joy of color even in times of war,
Chagall flew through the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a broken body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic strip.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds - now it's your turn to answer.

pt.original:
Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotion directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, layering thick paint, with brushstrokes swirling intensely.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his canvases.
Every brushstroke carries his passion and solitude.
pt.translated:
Van Gogh era um artista que expressava emoções diretamente através da cor.
Usou a técnica do impasto; a tinta espessa acumula-se em pinceladas intensamente giratórias.

Contrastes complementares ousados de amarelo e azul dominam as suas telas.
Cada pincelada carrega a sua paixão e solidão.
pt.back:
Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotions directly through color.
He used the impasto technique; thick paint accumulates in intensely swirling brushstrokes.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his canvases.
Each brushstroke carries his passion and loneliness.

pt.original:
Klimt was an artist who painted the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles, and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Beneath that gold, sensuality and divinity intertwine as one.
pt.translated:
Klimt era o artista que pintou os sonhos dourados da Viena fin-de-siècle.
Colou folhas de ouro reais sobre a tela, com padrões geométricos inspirados nos mosaicos bizantinos.

Espirais, círculos e triângulos criam ritmos decorativos por toda a superfície.
Sob aquele ouro, sensualidade e sacralidade fundem-se numa só coisa.
pt.back:
Klimt was the artist who painted the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He pasted real gold leaf onto the canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spirals, circles and triangles create decorative rhythms across the surface.
Under that gold, sensuality and sacredness merge into one.

pt.original:
Munch painted not what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through swirling curves and distorted forms.

Blood-red skies and sickly colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
From his brush, inner screams burst forth.
pt.translated:
Munch não pintou o que via, mas o que sentia.
Revelou a psicologia humana através de curvas retorcidas e formas distorcidas.

Céus ensanguentados e cores doentias visualizam a angústia existencial e o medo.
Do seu pincel, os gritos interiores explodem.
pt.back:
Munch didn't paint what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through twisted curves and distorted shapes.

Bloody skies and sickly colors visualize existential anguish and fear.
From his brush, inner screams explode.

pt.original:
Matisse was an artist who liberated color as the language of emotion.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly placing greens, purples, and oranges on faces.

Complex forms are daringly simplified into curves and flat planes.
In his paintings, color sings on its own.
pt.translated:
Matisse era o artista que libertou a cor como linguagem emocional.
Recusou as cores realistas, colocando verde, violeta e laranja ousadamente nos rostos.

Formas complexas são simplificadas com ousadia em curvas e planos.
Nas suas telas, a cor canta sozinha.
pt.back:
Matisse was the artist who liberated color as an emotional language.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly putting green, violet and orange on faces.

Complex shapes are boldly simplified into curves and planes.
On his canvases, color sings alone.

pt.original:
Chagall was an artist who captured love and dreams on canvas.
He painted worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue wrap around memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.
pt.translated:
Chagall era o artista que capturou amor e sonhos sobre a tela.
Pintou os mundos da memória e da fantasia com cores oníricas e composições surreais.

Rosa, violeta suave e azul cobalto envolvem as memórias da sua terra natal, Vitebsk.
Nas suas telas, realidade e sonhos entrelaçam-se sem fronteiras.
pt.back:
Chagall was the artist who captured love and dreams on canvas.
He painted the worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pinks, soft violets and cobalt blues envelop the memories of his native Vitebsk.
On his canvases, reality and dreams intertwine without borders.

pt.original:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founder of Cubism.
He deconstructed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple viewpoints on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where front and side views exist simultaneously.
He dismantled 500 years of perspective's established order.
pt.translated:
Picasso era o maior revolucionário do século 20, fundador do Cubismo.
Decompôs os objetos em planos geométricos e remontou múltiplos pontos de vista numa só tela.

A sua marca é a estrutura multi-perspéctica onde rosto e perfil coexistem ao mesmo tempo.
Destruiu a ordem estabelecida de 500 anos de perspetiva.
pt.back:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, the founder of Cubism.
He decomposed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple points of view on a single canvas.

His trademark is the multi-perspective structure where face and profile coexist at the same time.
He destroyed the established order of 500 years of perspective.

pt.original:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who transformed pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Red, yellow, and green Mexican folk colors dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.
pt.translated:
Frida Kahlo era uma artista que transformou a dor em arte.
Enfrentou a si mesma com um olhar intenso e elementos simbólicos.

Vermelho, amarelo e verde das cores do folclore mexicano dominam as suas telas.
Nas suas pinturas, dor e existência tornam-se uma só coisa.
pt.back:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who turned pain into art.
She faced herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

The red, yellow and green colors of Mexican folklore dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.

pt.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged Ben-Day dots from print media and fixed forms with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill his canvases.
He broke down the boundary between popular culture and fine art.
pt.translated:
Lichtenstein era o mestre do pop art que elevou os quadrinhos às belas-artes.
Ampliou os pontos Ben-Day dos media impressos e fixou as formas com contornos pretos espessos.

Apenas cores primárias puras — vermelho, azul e amarelo — preenchem as suas telas.
Apagou a fronteira entre cultura popular e belas-artes.
pt.back:
Lichtenstein was the master of pop art who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged the Ben-Day dots of printed media and fixed the shapes with thick black outlines.

Only pure primary colors - red, blue and yellow - fill his canvases.
He erased the boundary between popular culture and fine art.

th.translated:
7 ปรมาจารย์ ก่อนที่พวกเขาจะเป็นอัจฉริยะ
พวกเขาเป็นคนที่ออกเดินทางค้นหาภาษาของตนเอง

ฟาน โกะห์ทิ้ง 900 ชิ้นไว้ใน 10 ปีแล้วจากไป
ดาวของเขายังคงหมุนวนอยู่

คลิมท์ซ่อนรักและความตายไว้ใต้ทองคำ
มุงก์คร่ำครวญเสียงร้องแห่งการสูญเสียตลอดชีวิต

มาติสปกป้องความยินดีแห่งสีสันแม้กลางเปลวไฟสงคราม
ชาร์กาลบินอยู่กลางอากาศกับคนที่รัก

ฟรีดาเผชิญกับตัวเองด้วยร่างกายที่แตกสลาย
ลิกเทนสไตน์ตั้งคำถามกับโลกด้วยช่องการ์ตูนช่องเดียว

พวกเขาตั้งคำถาม ทำลาย และสร้างใหม่อีกครั้ง
เจ็ดโลก ตอนนี้ถึงคราวของคุณตอบ
th.back:
7 Masters Before They Became Geniuses, They Were People Who Set Out to Find Their Own Language. Van Gogh left behind 900 pieces in 10 years before departing. His star continues to orbit. Klimt hid love and death beneath gold. Munch lamented the cry of loss throughout his life.

Matisse embraces the joy of color even amidst the flames of war. Chagall flies through the air with his loved ones. Frida confronts herself with a broken body. Lichtenstein questions the world with a single comic panel. They question, destroy, and create anew. Seven worlds. Now it's your turn to respond.

Van Gogh was an artist who expressed emotion directly through color.
He used the impasto technique, layering thick paint, with brushstrokes swirling intensely.

Bold complementary contrasts of yellow and blue dominate his canvases.
Every brushstroke carries his passion and solitude.
th.translated:
ฟาน โกะห์ คือจิตรกรที่แสดงอารมณ์โดยตรงผ่านสีสัน
ใช้เทคนิคอิมพาสโตที่ทาสีหนาซ้อนกัน และพู่กันเคลื่อนไหวหมุนวนอย่างรุนแรง

สีเหลืองและน้ำเงินที่คมชัดอย่างรุนแรงครอบครองภาพ
ในทุกรอยพู่กัน ความหลงใหลและความเดียวดายของเขาสถิตอยู่โดยตรง
th.back: Van Gogh was a painter who expressed emotions directly through color, using impasto techniques with thick, layered brushstrokes and intense, swirling movements. Sharp, vivid yellows and blues dominate his paintings. In every stroke, his passion and solitude are directly present.

th.original:
Klimt was an artist who painted the golden dreams of fin-de-siècle Vienna.
He applied real gold leaf to canvas, with geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics.

Spiral curves, circles, and triangles create decorative rhythms across the entire surface.
Beneath that gold, sensuality and divinity intertwine as one.
th.translated:
คลิมท์ คือจิตรกรผู้วาดความฝันสีทองของเวียนนาปลายศตวรรษ
นำทองคำเปลวจริงมาใช้บนผืนผ้าใบ และลวดลายเรขาคณิตจากแรงบันดาลใจของโมเสกไบแซนไทน์ซ้ำๆ

เส้นโค้งรูปก้นหอย วงกลม และสามเหลี่ยมสร้างจังหวะตกแต่งทั่วภาพ
ใต้สีทองนั้น กามารมณ์และความศักดิ์สิทธิ์พันกันเป็นหนึ่ง
th.back: Klimt is the painter who captured the golden dreams of late-century Vienna, using real gold leaf on canvas and geometric patterns inspired by Byzantine mosaics. Spiral curves, circles, and triangles create decorative rhythms throughout the images. Beneath the golden hues, sensuality and sacredness intertwine as one.

th.original:
Munch painted not what he saw, but what he felt.
He revealed human psychology through swirling curves and distorted forms.

Blood-red skies and sickly colors visualize existential anxiety and fear.
From his brush, inner screams burst forth.
th.translated:
มุงก์ คือจิตรกรผู้วาดสิ่งที่รู้สึก ไม่ใช่สิ่งที่เห็น
เปิดเผยจิตวิทยาของมนุษย์โดยตรงด้วยเส้นโค้งหมุนวนและรูปทรงที่บิดเบือน

ท้องฟ้าสีเลือดและสีที่เป็นโรคทำให้ความวิตกและความหวาดกลัวแห่งการดำรงอยู่เป็นภาพ
จากปลายพู่กันของเขาเสียงร้องจากภายในพุ่งออกมา
th.back: Munch is a painter who depicts what is felt, not what is seen. He reveals human psychology directly through swirling curves and distorted forms. Blood-red skies and diseased colors transform anxiety and the fear of existence into images. From the tip of his brush, cries from within burst forth.

th.original:
Matisse was an artist who liberated color as the language of emotion.
He rejected realistic colors, boldly placing greens, purples, and oranges on faces.

Complex forms are daringly simplified into curves and flat planes.
In his paintings, color sings on its own.
th.translated:
มาติส คือจิตรกรผู้ปลดปล่อยสีสันให้เป็นภาษาของอารมณ์
ไม่ยึดติดกับสีจริง และวางสีเขียว ม่วง หรือส้มบนใบหน้าโดยไม่ลังเล

รูปทรงซับซ้อนถูกละเว้นอย่างกล้าหาญเป็นเส้นโค้งและระนาบง่ายๆ
บนภาพของเขา สีสันร้องเพลงออกมาเอง
th.back: Matisse is a painter who liberated colors into the language of emotions, not bound by true colors, and placed green, purple, or orange on faces without hesitation. Complex shapes are boldly omitted, reduced to simple curves and planes in his paintings. The colors sing out on their own.

th.original:
Chagall was an artist who captured love and dreams on canvas.
He painted worlds of memory and fantasy with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions.

Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue wrap around memories of his hometown Vitebsk.
In his paintings, reality and dreams blend without boundaries.
th.translated:
ชาร์กาล คือจิตรกรผู้วาดความรักและความฝันบนผืนผ้าใบ
วาดโลกแห่งความทรงจำและจินตนาการด้วยสีฝันและการจัดวางที่ไม่สมจริง

ชมพู ม่วงอ่อน และน้ำเงินโคบอลต์โอบล้อมความทรงจำของบ้านเกิดวีเตบสก์
บนภาพของเขา ความจริงและความฝันละลายกันโดยไม่มีเส้นแบ่ง
th.back: Chagall is a painter who depicts love and dreams on canvas, creating a world of memories and imagination with dreamlike colors and surreal compositions. Pink, soft purple, and cobalt blue envelop the memories of his hometown, Vitebsk. In his paintings, reality and fantasy blend together without any clear boundaries.

th.original:
Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, founder of Cubism.
He deconstructed objects into geometric planes and reassembled multiple viewpoints on a single canvas.

His signature is the multi-perspective structure where front and side views exist simultaneously.
He dismantled 500 years of perspective's established order.
th.translated:
ปิกัสโซ คือนักปฏิวัติผู้ยิ่งใหญ่ที่สุดแห่งศตวรรษที่ 20 ผู้ก่อตั้งคิวบิสม์
แยกหัวข้อออกเป็นระนาบเรขาคณิตและจัดวางมุมมองหลายมุมบนผืนผ้าใบเดียว

โครงสร้างมุมมองหลายมุมที่ด้านหน้าและด้านข้างอยู่พร้อมกันเป็นลักษณะเฉพาะ
เขาถอดแยกระเบียบมุมมอง 500 ปี
th.back: Picasso was the greatest revolutionary of the 20th century, the founder of Cubism, who broke subjects down into geometric planes and arranged multiple perspectives on a single canvas. The distinctive feature is the simultaneous presence of frontal and side views. He dismantled the 500-year-old perspective system.

th.original:
Frida Kahlo was an artist who transformed pain into art.
She confronted herself with an intense gaze and symbolic elements.

Red, yellow, and green Mexican folk colors dominate her canvases.
In her paintings, pain and existence become one.
th.translated:
ฟรีดา คาห์โล คือจิตรกรผู้ยกระดับความเจ็บปวดสู่งานศิลปะ
เผชิญกับตัวเองด้วยสายตาตรงหน้าอย่างรุนแรงและองค์ประกอบเชิงสัญลักษณ์

แดง เหลือง และเขียว สีพื้นบ้านเม็กซิโกครอบครองภาพ
บนผืนผ้าใบของเธอ ความเจ็บปวดและการดำรงอยู่กลายเป็นหนึ่ง
th.back: Frida Kahlo is a painter who elevated pain into art, confronting herself with a direct and intense gaze and symbolic elements. Red, yellow, and green—the vibrant colors of Mexico—dominate her canvases. On her canvas, pain and existence become one.

th.original:
Lichtenstein was a Pop Art master who elevated comics to fine art.
He enlarged Ben-Day dots from print media and fixed forms with bold black outlines.

Only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—fill his canvases.
He broke down the boundary between popular culture and fine art.
th.translated:
ลิกเทนสไตน์ คือปรมาจารย์ป็อปอาร์ตผู้ยกระดับการ์ตูนสู่ศิลปะ
ขยาย Ben-Day Dots แบบสิ่งพิมพ์ให้ยักษ์ใหญ่ และตรึงรูปทรงด้วยเส้นขอบดำหนา

สีปฐมภูมิบริสุทธิ์แดง น้ำเงิน เหลืองเพียงอย่างเดียวเติมเต็มภาพ
เขาทำลายเส้นแบ่งระหว่างวัฒนธรรมมวลชนและศิลปะบริสุทธิ์
th.back: Lichtenstein is a pop art master who elevated cartoons to the realm of art. He expanded Ben-Day dots from print media to monumental sizes and anchored shapes with thick black outlines. He used only pure primary colors—red, blue, and yellow—to complete his images. He broke down the boundaries between mass culture and fine art.

th.translated: วินเซนต์ ฟาน โกะห์ (Vincent van Gogh, 1853–1890)
th.back: Vincent van Gogh (1853–1890)

th.translated: คืนแห่งดวงดาว · ทานตะวัน · ภาพเหมือนตนเอง
th.back: Night of the Stars · Sunflower · Self-Portrait

th.translated: ความหลงใหลจากปลายพู่กันที่หมุนวน
th.back: The Passion from the Swirling Brushstroke

th.translated: กุสตาฟ คลิมท์ (Gustav Klimt, 1862–1918)
th.back: Gustav Klimt (1862–1918)

th.original: The Kiss · Judith · The Tree of Life
th.translated: จูบ · จูดิธ · ต้นไม้แห่งชีวิต
th.back: Kiss · Judith · Tree of Life

th.translated: โลกแห่งประสาทสัมผัสสีทอง
th.back: The Golden Sensory World

th.translated: เอดวาร์ด มุงก์ (Edvard Munch, 1863–1944)
th.back: Edvard Munch (1863–1944)

th.original: The Scream · Madonna · The Dance of Life
th.translated: เสียงร้อง · มาดอนนา · การเต้นรำแห่งชีวิต
th.back: The Voice · Madonna · The Dance of Life

th.translated: วาดเสียงร้องจากภายใน
th.back: Draw the sound from within.

th.translated: อองรี มาติส (Henri Matisse, 1869–1954)
th.back: Henri Matisse (1869–1954)

th.original: The Dance · The Red Room · The Green Stripe
th.translated: การเต้นรำ · ห้องสีแดง · แถบสีเขียว
th.back: Dancing · Red Room · Green Strip

th.translated: นักมายากลแห่งสีสัน
th.back: The Magician of Colors

th.translated: มาร์ก ชาร์กาล (Marc Chagall, 1887–1985)
th.back: Marc Chagall (1887–1985)

th.original: Over the Town · I and the Village · Bouquet with Lovers
th.translated: เหนือเมือง · ฉันกับหมู่บ้าน · ช่อดอกไม้กับคู่รัก
th.back: Above the City · Me and the Village · Bouquet with Lovers

th.translated: กวีแห่งความรักและความฝัน
th.back: Poet of Love and Dreams

th.translated: ปาโบล ปิกัสโซ (Pablo Picasso, 1881–1973)
th.back: Pablo Picasso (1881–1973)

th.original: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon · Guernica · Portrait of Dora Maar
th.translated: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon · Guernica · ภาพเหมือนดอรา มาร์
th.back: Les Demoiselles d'Avignon · Guernica · Portrait of Dora Maar

th.translated: นักปฏิวัติผู้ถอดแยกสายตา
th.back: The revolutionary who dismantles the gaze.

th.translated: ฟรีดา คาห์โล (Frida Kahlo, 1907–1954)
th.back: Frida Kahlo (1907–1954)

th.original: The Broken Column · Self-Portrait with Thorn Necklace
th.translated: กระดูกสันหลังที่หัก · ภาพเหมือนกับสร้อยหนามและนกฮัมมิ่งเบิร์ด
th.back: Broken spine · An image resembling a thorny necklace and a hummingbird

th.translated: ภาพเหมือนตนเองที่จ้องมองความเจ็บปวด
th.back: A self-portrait gazing at pain

th.translated: รอย ลิกเทนสไตน์ (Roy Lichtenstein, 1923–1997)
th.back: Roy Lichtenstein (1923–1997)

th.original: Drowning Girl · Whaam! · In the Car
th.translated: Drowning Girl · Whaam! · In the Car
th.back: Drowning Girl · Whaam! · In the Car

th.translated: ชายผู้เปลี่ยนการ์ตูนเป็นศิลปะ
th.back: The Man Who Turned Cartoons into Art

th.original:
Seven masters—before they were geniuses,
they were wanderers searching for their own language.

Van Gogh left 900 works in 10 years,
and his stars still swirl today.

Klimt hid love and death beneath gold,
Munch screamed his loss throughout his life.

Matisse kept the joy of color even through war,
Chagall flew through the sky with his beloved.

Frida faced herself with a broken body,
Lichtenstein questioned the world with a single comic panel.

They asked, they broke, they rebuilt.
Seven worlds—now it's your turn to respond.
th.translated:
7 ปรมาจารย์ ก่อนที่พวกเขาจะเป็นอัจฉริยะ
พวกเขาเป็นคนที่ออกเดินทางค้นหาภาษาของตนเอง

ฟาน โกะห์ทิ้ง 900 ชิ้นไว้ใน 10 ปีแล้วจากไป
ดาวของเขายังคงหมุนวนอยู่

คลิมท์ซ่อนรักและความตายไว้ใต้ทองคำ
มุงก์คร่ำครวญเสียงร้องแห่งการสูญเสียตลอดชีวิต

มาติสปกป้องความยินดีแห่งสีสันแม้กลางเปลวไฟสงคราม
ชาร์กาลบินอยู่กลางอากาศกับคนที่รัก

ฟรีดาเผชิญกับตัวเองด้วยร่างกายที่แตกสลาย
ลิกเทนสไตน์ตั้งคำถามกับโลกด้วยช่องการ์ตูนช่องเดียว

พวกเขาตั้งคำถาม ทำลาย และสร้างใหม่อีกครั้ง
เจ็ดโลก ตอนนี้ถึงคราวของคุณตอบ
th.back:
7 Masters Before They Became Geniuses, They Were People Who Set Out to Find Their Own Language. Van Gogh left behind 900 pieces in 10 years before departing. His star continues to orbit. Klimt hid love and death beneath gold. Munch lamented the cry of loss throughout his life.

Matisse embraces the joy of color even amidst the flames of war. Chagall flies through the air with his loved ones. Frida confronts herself with a broken body. Lichtenstein questions the world with a single comic panel. They question, destroy, and create anew. Seven worlds. Now it's your turn to respond.
